prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='catboost', seasonality_champion='catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.558) and high seasonality variance (0.8785), indicating models capture patterns very differently. catboost dominates both trend tracking and seasonality capture, while ARIMA excels at late horizons. The varying true trend directions (down→up→up) combined with strong seasonal amplitude (12.56) suggest DBA combination is optimal to leverage model diversity without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "catboost",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "catboost", "late": "ARIMA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "THETA", "NaiveMovingAverage", "rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ETS"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.558,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.558) and high seasonality variance (0.8785), indicating models capture patterns very differently. catboost dominates both trend tracking and seasonality capture, while ARIMA excels at late horizons. The varying true trend directions (down→up→up) combined with strong seasonal amplitude (12.56) suggest DBA combination is optimal to leverage model diversity without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 2 baseline (dba_combination, robust_median), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All types represented. Scores are competitive with universe leaderboards - best_per_horizon_by_validation (0.891) and robust_median (0.875) rank high. No leakage issues detected (all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding or are baselines). n_windows=3 meets threshold for weighted candidates. Set size=5 is appropriate, no redundancy requiring pruning.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["best_single_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.70"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.70 with higher shrinkage (0.70) for stability. best_single_by_validation has lowest RMSE (12.75) in universe leaderboard. Current set already well-diversified with DBA, weighted, selection, and robust methods - no removals needed.", "changes": ["added best_single_by_validation for lowest RMSE in universe", "added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.70 with higher shrinkage for low n_windows"], "when_good": "n_windows >= 6 would allow ridge_stacking to become viable"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.558) and high seasonality variance (0.8785), indicating models capture patterns very differently. catboost dominates both trend tracking and seasonality capture, while ARIMA excels at late horizons. The varying true trend directions (down→up→up) combined with strong seasonal amplitude (12.56) suggest DBA combination is optimal to leverage model diversity without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.552 (≥0.25) signals strong disagreement favoring robust_median and dba_combination. n_windows=3 is very low, so shrinkage=0.35 on inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.8785) and varying trend directions (down→up→up). RMSE spread_ratio=0.558 justifies rmse_focus preset.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 2 baseline (dba_combination, robust_median), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). All types represented. Scores are competitive with universe leaderboards - best_per_horizon_by_validation (0.891) and robust_median (0.875) rank high. No leakage issues detected (all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding or are baselines). n_windows=3 meets threshold for weighted candidates. Set size=5 is appropriate, no redundancy requiring pruning.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.70 with higher shrinkage (0.70) for stability. best_single_by_validation has lowest RMSE (12.75) in universe leaderboard. Current set already well-diversified with DBA, weighted, selection, and robust methods - no removals needed.
changes: added best_single_by_validation for lowest RMSE in universe
added inverse_rmse_weights_k4_sh0.70 with higher shrinkage for low n_windows
statistician.when_good: n_windows >= 6 would allow ridge_stacking to become viable
